mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate, reflecting a restrictive monetary policy stance, increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions. This direct valuation pressure leads to negative forward returns on the S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% is the highest since 2001.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is positive during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate signals a late-cycle economy where labor market tightness puts upward pressure on wages, squeezing corporate profit margins. This anticipates an economic peak and subsequent slowdown, causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.4% is near the lows of the current economic cycle.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained negative trend in housing starts is a leading indicator of weakening aggregate demand, particularly for durable goods and related services. This signals a future decline in corporate revenues, leading to negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has declined significantly from its peak in early 2006.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month momentum of HOUST and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and corporate capital expenditure. This indicates broad economic strength and supports higher corporate earnings, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) for INDPRO 3-month change against 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS) and a tight labor market (low UNRATE) signals a high probability of a policy-induced slowdown. The central bank is actively braking an overheating economy, a condition that is historically bearish for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current conditions of FEDFUNDS=5.25% and UNRATE=4.4% meet the trigger.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not underperform its unconditional average return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) when both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth indicates strong current consumer spending, which is the largest component of GDP. This positive real-time signal of economic health leads to upward revisions of near-term corporate earnings, boosting equity prices over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (percent of times the S&P 500 has a positive 5-day return when triggered) is less than 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, unexpectedly strong payrolls growth is interpreted negatively by markets ('good news is bad news'). It signals persistent economic momentum that will require the central bank to maintain or increase its restrictive policy stance, raising discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has been in a tightening cycle for over a year.
failure_condition: When the 6-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive, instances of PAYEMS growth > 175k are not followed by negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A high and persistent rate of consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins through higher input costs and pressures the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance. This combination of lower real earnings and higher discount rates is negative for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation has been volatile and above 3.5% for parts of 2006.
failure_condition: The average real 60-day S&P 500 return is non-negative during periods when year-over-year CPI inflation is above 3.5%.
